task: I had a customer ask for a refund on a card charge after already leaving, and I want to handle it the right way without getting burned. What should I keep on hand or write down if I need to protect myself later?

turns.agent_response:
Keep a tight paper trail and make the refund decision based on what the record shows.

What to keep on hand:
- Receipt or check copy
- Card slip / signed authorization if you have it
- POS record showing the original charge
- Refund/void record if you issue one
- Manager approval, if your place requires it
- Any note tied to the guest complaint
- Camera reference if your system allows it
- Shift log or incident log entry

What to write down:
- Date and time
- Guest name, if known
- Last 4 digits of the card, if allowed by policy
- Exact item or charge amount in question
- What the guest said, using their exact words if possible
- What you found in the record
- What action you took: refunded, denied, partial, or escalated
- Who approved it
- Your initials

Best practice:
- Verify the charge first before talking policy.
- Repeat the exact amount back to the guest.
- If the record backs the charge, keep it short and stick to that.
- If you do refund, make sure the refund matches the original charge and is documented.

If you want, I can give you a simple incident-log template you can use at work.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s basically the right paper trail. Receipt, POS record, refund record if it happens, manager sign-off, and a quick incident note. Last 4 only if policy allows it — not trying to collect extra info just because somebody’s whining.

I’d also keep:
- exact time the card was run
- server name / section
- table number or ticket number
- the reason they gave for the refund
- whether they called, came back, or went through the card company
- any screenshots if the system shows the charge history

The big thing is tying the complaint to the original ticket fast. If it’s a card dispute later, paper beats memory every time. I’ve had people try to “forget” what they ordered or claim they never got half the stuff. Receipts shut that down.

If you want, I can write you a tight incident-log format you can just copy by hand when it happens.
